title: T-121 — Global Import / Export screen (SET-029)
prompt:
# T-121 — Global Import / Export screen (SET-029)

## Goal
SET-029 global Import / Export production screen, in scope per PO 2026-05-03.

Canonical Settings prototype source of truth is `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/*.jsx`. If an exact screen prototype is missing, implement spec-driven from `prototypes/design/02-SETTINGS-UX.md` and reuse available prototype elements/patterns only where they match the UX contract. No exact canonical prototype exists under `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/*.jsx`; UX SET-029 is authoritative. Root legacy `prototypes/settings/import-export.jsx` may be used only as non-canonical pattern reference if helpful. This is global Settings Import/Export, not only reference table CSV T-096.

## Files in scope
- apps/web/app/(admin)/settings/import-export/page.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(admin)/settings/import-export/page.test.tsx [create]

## Acceptance criteria (all must hold)
- Given /settings/import-export renders, then it follows UX SET-029: Import card, Export card, entity selector, upload/dropzone, template download, export format choices, recent jobs/status area, loading/empty/error states.
- Given a supported entity is selected for import, when a CSV is uploaded, then the user is routed into the relevant preview/commit flow or sees a clear unsupported-entity message; Reference entity reuses T-096/T-022.
- Given export is requested, then the UI calls server export action for the selected entity/format and shows download/error status; do not conflate this with per-reference-table CSV only.

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: RTL test for two-card layout, entity selection, reference import route handoff, export action call.
- GREEN: build screen and wire existing import/export actions where available.
- Run page test, typecheck, lint.

## Risk red lines
- Global Import/Export is Phase 1 scope per PO 2026-05-03; do not mark it out-of-scope or Phase 3.
- Do not claim 1:1 canonical prototype parity; exact design-system prototype is missing.

## Depth patch — Global Import/Export permission and entity depth
- This is the global Settings Import/Export hub, not only Reference CSV. Entity selector must include users, roles, invitations, reference tables, infrastructure, feature flags, and authorization policies.
- Each entity declares import/export support, required permission(s), template availability, sync vs async behavior, and audit requirements.
- Imports that mutate authorization policies require `settings.authorization.edit`, an explicit audit reason, dry-run validation through T-122, and must never bypass V-SET-43/V-SET-44 invariants.
- Export is read-only and permission-filtered; unsupported imports are clearly marked export-only/unsupported instead of silently reusing reference CSV.
- Tests must cover permission-filtered entity list, reference handoff to T-096/T-022, unsupported import messaging, authorization-policy dry-run blocker, and recent job statuses.

Depth patch 2026-05-03: ## Depth patch — Global Import/Export permission and entity depth - This is the global Settings Import/Export hub, not only Reference CSV. Entity selector must include users, roles, invitations, reference tables, infrastructure, feature flags, and authorization policies. - Each entity declares import/export support, required permission(s), template availability, sync vs async behavior, and audit requirements. - Imports that mutate authorization policies require `settings.authorization.edit`, an explicit audit reason, dry-run validation through T-122, and must never bypass V-SET-43/V-SET-44 invariants. - Export is read-only and permission-filtered; unsupported imports are clearly marked export-only/unsupported instead of silently reusing reference CSV. - Tests must cover permission-filtered entity list, reference handoff to T-096/T-022, unsupported import messaging, authorization-policy dry-run blocker, and recent job statuses.
Preserved acceptance requirements: Entity selector explicitly distinguishes Settings entities (users, roles, reference tables, feature flags, authorization policies) and marks unsupported imports as read-only/export-only instead of silently routing to reference CSV.; Entity selector includes users, roles, invitations, reference tables, infrastructure, feature flags, and authorization policies with per-entity import/export capability labels.; Authorization policy import requires settings.authorization.edit, audit reason, and T-122 dry-run/preflight; V-SET-43/V-SET-44 cannot be bypassed by CSV import.; RTL tests cover permission-filtered entities, unsupported import states, reference handoff, authorization-policy blocker, and recent jobs/status area.

Additional acceptance detail preserved from compressed AC list:
- Additional preserved requirements in details are implemented and covered by targeted tests.
- UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.

Given a supported entity is selected for import, when a CSV is uploaded, then the user is routed into the relevant preview/commit flow or sees a clear unsupported-entity message; Reference entity reuses T-096/T-022.
Given export is requested, then the UI calls server export action for the selected entity/format and shows download/error status; do not conflate this with per-reference-table CSV only.

Given the task is closed out, when reviewers inspect the task evidence, then these additional requirements are proven together: Additional preserved requirements in details are implemented and covered by targeted tests.; UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.
test_strategy: RED: RTL test for two-card layout, entity selection, reference import route handoff, export action call.
GREEN: build screen and wire existing import/export actions where available.
Add RTL tests for per-entity permission filtering, authorization-policy import dry-run blocker, unsupported import message, and recent job statuses.
For UI parity, capture screenshots/artifacts and Playwright trace/video/artifacts where applicable; attach artifact paths in closeout.
risk_red_lines: Global Import/Export is Phase 1 scope per PO 2026-05-03; do not mark it out-of-scope or Phase 3.
Do not claim 1:1 canonical prototype parity; exact design-system prototype is missing.
Do not allow import to mutate authorization policies without `settings.authorization.edit` and audit reason.
Do not implement global import as a thin alias for reference CSV only.
Do not allow CSV import to mutate authorization policies without policy permissions, audit reason, and preflight validation.
